caption: Individual-soldier collision (foundation). Each regiment now runs a parallel layer of simulated soldier BODIES, overlaid here as debug dots: the engaged front ranks that run the full per-soldier pass are magenta, the formation-following bulk is cyan. As the lines clash, the magenta front-rank soldiers of the two enemy regiments press into each other and separate per-soldier across the contact face — the new cross-regiment soldier collision. The layer is non-authoritative for now (movement and combat still use the regiment circle), so nothing about how the battle plays has changed yet.
